other_observers: Cole Wild; first found the day before by Christian Nunes
description: the bird was a medium sized larid, a bit larger than Ring-billed Gulls, closer in size to California Gull. The overall shape was similar to that of a California Gull, but bulkier, with proportionally broader wings and a bulkier torso. The tail was a bit longer than that of a gull, and slightly wedge shaped, with the two central tail feathers projecting slightly beyond the rest of the tail. The bill was quite heavy, with a strong gonydeal angle about 2/3 of the way from the base of the bill to the tip. The base of the bill was pinkish gray in color, the nail was black.  The overall color of the bird was warm, choclatey brown. The upperwings had a broad white flash at the base of the primaries. The uppertail coverts were mostly brown, with some small white checks. The underwings were boldly checked with white. The underside of the primaries had a bold white base; the primary coverts on the underwing were also white, creating a "double flash" when combined with the white undersides of the primaries. The undertail coverts were heavily barred black and white.Behaviors: The bird was first observed flying around about 30 feet over the water. It eventually landed near the middle of the lake and spent the rest of the observation swimming out there.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenal intermediate morph
